_meta.title: A2A protocol core (B1 wave) — @agent-ui/a2a substrate decomposition
_meta.author: system-planner
_meta.refines: a2a-section.decomp.json (core + tools regions, re-cut at module granularity for the B1 LLD)
_meta.note: OUTSIDE-IN = the package structure (src/protocol <- src/rpc <- src/channel <- tools/, downward-only). INSIDE-OUT = the needs, derived from SPEC-R2..R8 + the tic-tac-toe LLD's consumption points (loopback channel, shared validator, lifecycle input-required arm, three cards at the well-known path). Two field-level upstream shapes surfaced as residual HV gaps (TaskStatus/Artifact internals; method params/results) — modeled as a grounding node gating the slices that type them (SPEC-R1).

nodes.label: residual grounding deltas (HV-11/HV-12 rows added to SPEC §2)
nodes.accept: SPEC §2 rows HV-11 + HV-12 each carry a verbatim upstream citation + resolution date (host fetch) before the slices that type them (S2/S5) dispatch; the B1 dispatch review records it

nodes.label: package scaffold + gate wiring (package.json, root tsconfig paths, exports map)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green with the new package present (root include/vitest globs pick up packages/agent-ui/a2a/src); exports map exposes '.' only; zero dependencies in package.json

nodes.label: src/protocol/ — wire model + judgment (bottom layer, imports nothing)
typed wire model (Message/Part/Task/TaskStatus/AgentCard; literal-union TaskState)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` green; the fixture-transcription test (every committed fixture parses into the typed model) exits 0; TaskState is the 9-member union, terminals a 4-member const

nodes.label: byte-fidelity codec (decode = parse+judge, encode = order-preserving stringify)
nodes.accept: round-trip test: decode→encode byte-identical over every committed fixture (SPEC-R3 AC1); a non-canonical fixture fails at commit time; exits 0

nodes.label: total validator validateA2a (batch, path-addressed, closed 5-code set)
nodes.accept: malformed-input corpus: 0 throws; one firing fixture + one passing sibling per code (A2A_SCHEMA/PIN/CARD/RPC via validateA2a, A2A_STATE via the lifecycle guard — same failure type); exits 0 (SPEC-R6 AC1, R3 AC2)

nodes.label: task lifecycle transition table + guard (family policy over the HV-5 state set)
nodes.accept: the full 9×9 (state × proposed-next) matrix test: every legal pair advances, every illegal pair yields A2A_STATE, the 4 terminals have zero outgoing edges (SPEC-R4 AC1); exits 0

nodes.label: src/rpc/ — JSON-RPC 2.0 framing over the protocol layer
error-code table + wire-error mapping (5 JSON-RPC std + 7 A2A codes, HV-9)
nodes.accept: bidirectional mapping test over all 12 codes; an unknown inbound code maps to the declared fallback, never a throw; exits 0

nodes.label: envelope framing/parsing + correlation (message/send · tasks/get · tasks/cancel)
nodes.accept: per-method frame→parse round-trip with correlation intact; malformed-envelope fixtures yield A2A_RPC, no throw (SPEC-R7 AC1); unknown method maps -32601, known-unsupported maps -32004; exits 0

nodes.label: src/channel/ — A2aChannel contract + in-proc loopback pair (deterministic, CI-first)
nodes.accept: ordered-delivery test (interleaved sends decode in order), close semantics (iterators end; send-after-close rejects typed), zero timers/network (SPEC-R8 AC1 loopback arm, N5); exits 0

nodes.label: tools/ — Node dev scope (never in a consumer bundle)
HTTP JSON-RPC transport (handleRpc core + thin node:http shell + client channel)
nodes.accept: transport-invariance test: identical A2aMessage sequence decoded over loopback and the HTTP framing path, socket-free at test time (SPEC-R8 AC1); consumer exports carry no tools path

nodes.label: agent-card serving + discovery client (/.well-known/agent-card.json)
nodes.accept: the arena's three card fixtures validate when served/fetched; a missing-required-field card yields A2A_CARD at its path and is refused for discovery (SPEC-R5 AC1); exits 0

nodes.label: committed wire fixtures (messages/tasks/cards/envelopes, transcribed from HV resolutions)
nodes.accept: every fixture pins protocolVersion 0.3.0 and passes validateA2a clean; a standing test re-validates every committed fixture (SPEC-R2); exits 0

actions.label: resolve the residual field-level upstream shapes (TaskStatus/Artifact internals; method params/results) before typing them
represent messages + parts at v0.3.0 fidelity (kind discriminators; FileWithBytes|FileWithUri mutually exclusive)
represent tasks + the full 9-member state set with the 4 terminals named
represent the agent card carrying BOTH protocolVersion and version (never conflated)
re-encode any decoded wire artifact byte-identically
judge any wire artifact totally — batch, coded, path-addressed, never a throw
refuse an unsupported protocolVersion pin with A2A_PIN, never silently proceed
advance legal lifecycle transitions; reject illegal ones with A2A_STATE; admit no terminal exit
frame a correlated request/response per supported method with deterministic id allocation
refuse a malformed or unknown-method envelope with a coded failure, never a throw
map every A2A + JSON-RPC error code to/from typed failures, both directions
carry ordered messages between two in-proc endpoints deterministically (no timers, no network)
carry the same envelopes over HTTP in dev with unchanged message semantics
serve a validated agent card at the well-known path; refuse to serve an invalid one
discover a peer by fetching + validating its card; an invalid card is never used
pin the verified upstream shapes as committed fixtures the tests assert against
run under the repo's standing gates (check + test) from the first slice
expose the zero-dep consumer surface only; dev transports stay out of it

edges.why: the types transcribe the HV-11 field-level shapes — typing before resolution bakes guesses into the core (SPEC-R1)
params/result shapes for the three methods are HV-12 — framing types them
fixtures are transcriptions of resolved rows; an unresolved row has nothing faithful to transcribe
modules land inside the wired package so check+test bite from the first real slice
the fixture-transcription test parses fixtures into the typed model
the validator judges the typed shapes
the transition table is typed over the TaskState union
envelopes carry the typed message/task union as params/results
the channel's send/receive signature carries the typed messages
decode composes the shared validator — a decode that skips judgment forks SPEC-R6
the byte-fidelity acceptance asserts against the committed canonical fixtures
framing maps wire errors through the shared code table — one table, no fork
the HTTP transport carries framed envelopes; it owns no framing of its own
transport invariance is asserted against the loopback channel contract
serving and discovery judge the card through the shared validator (SPEC-N4, no fork)
